tasks.spec: Write a single haiku (three lines, 5-7-5 feel) about many small AI agents working in parallel under one orchestrator. Write it to a file named haiku.txt in your current working directory, and write nothing else. The three lines must be plain text, one per line.
tasks.check: test -f /tmp/ringer-wall-demo/haiku/haiku.txt && lines=$(grep -c . /tmp/ringer-wall-demo/haiku/haiku.txt) && [ "$lines" -ge 3 ] && echo "haiku ok ($lines non-empty lines)" || { echo "FAIL: haiku.txt must have at least 3 non-empty lines"; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: haiku.txt exists with at least three non-empty lines

tasks.spec: Create a file fizzbuzz.py in your current working directory containing a function fizzbuzz(n) that returns the string 'Fizz' if n is divisible by 3, 'Buzz' if divisible by 5, 'FizzBuzz' if divisible by both, and str(n) otherwise. Python 3, stdlib only, include a short docstring. Create no other files.
tasks.check: cd /tmp/ringer-wall-demo/fizzbuzz && python3 -c "import fizzbuzz as f; assert f.fizzbuzz(3)=='Fizz'; assert f.fizzbuzz(5)=='Buzz'; assert f.fizzbuzz(15)=='FizzBuzz'; assert f.fizzbuzz(7)=='7'; print('fizzbuzz ok')" || { echo 'FAIL: fizzbuzz(n) contract not met'; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: fizzbuzz.py implements the exact Fizz/Buzz/FizzBuzz/number contract (executed)

tasks.spec: Create a file greeting.json in your current working directory: a JSON object with exactly two keys — "tool" set to the string "ringer" and "message" set to any short friendly greeting string. Valid JSON, create no other files.
tasks.check: cd /tmp/ringer-wall-demo/greeting && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('greeting.json')); assert d['tool']=='ringer'; assert isinstance(d['message'],str) and d['message']; print('greeting ok:', d['message'])" || { echo 'FAIL: greeting.json must have tool==ringer and a non-empty message'; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: greeting.json is valid JSON with tool==ringer and a non-empty message (executed)
